codeReview.summary: The new specs and health tests broadly match the requested scenarios, but the required unauthenticated GET /api/me coverage is not testing the real endpoint. auth-middleware.test duplicates a simplified /api/me handler instead of exercising packages/api/src/index.ts, so this feature does not fully satisfy the auth-middleware requirement.

issues.description: The GET /api/me test defines an inline stand-in route ('Replicate the /api/me route from index.ts') instead of exercising the real endpoint in packages/api/src/index.ts. Because the test never hits the actual handler at index.ts:71-90, it can still pass if the real /api/me route regresses, so the feature does not fully cover the required unauthorized /api/me behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The api-test-worker skill assumes features target standalone route modules, but this feature included /api/me, which lives in packages/api/src/index.ts. Without guidance for app-level endpoints, the worker copied the route logic into the test instead of exercising the real endpoint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/api-test-worker/SKILL.md says 'The route handlers already exist'; packages/api/src/__tests__/auth-middleware.test.ts:80-87 duplicates /api/me; the real handler is in packages/api/src/index.ts:71-90.

summary: Fail. The feature adds substantial specs/auth/health coverage, but the required unauthenticated GET /api/me assertion is implemented against a copied test-only route rather than the real app endpoint, leaving a coverage hole.
